mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of moving averages fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases, or the predictive power reverses sign during contraction periods without regime filtering.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly CPI inflation above 3.5% erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to yield positive 60-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial output and retail sales indicates a supply-demand mismatch and inventory buildup, which compresses corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within one week during downturns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The dual decline condition produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases during contraction periods, or the signal loses predictive power when applied outside contraction regimes.
